obligations.obligation:
In terms of Regulation 13 of the 2014 Tariff Regulations and Regulation 23 of the
2019 Tariff Regulations, the Plant and Machinery Cost to be considered for the
calculation of the Initial Spares shall exclude the IDC, IEDC, land cost and cost of civil
works.
obligations.affected_entity: Plant & Machinery Costs for Initial Spares Exclusion
obligations.short_summary: Exclude IDC, IEDC, land cost and civil works from Plant & Machinery costs in initial spares calculation.

obligations.obligation:
Therefore, in view of the above and as per the Initial Spare Discharge Statement,
the excess Initial Spares of ₹10.52 lakh and ₹172.21 lakh shall be reduced from the
ACE for the FYs 2020-21 and 2019-20, respectively, in respect of Asset-1.
obligations.short_summary: Reduction of excess Initial Spares for Asset-1 in specific fiscal years.

obligations.obligation:
Similarly,
in respect of Asset-2, the excess Initial Spares of ₹39.51 lakh, ₹39.50 lakh, ₹19.75
lakh and ₹2.33 lakh shall be reduced from the ACE for the FYs 2022-23, 2021-22,
2020-21, respectively and from the capital cost as on COD.
obligations.short_summary: Excess Initial Spares to be reduced from ACE for FYs.

obligations.obligation:
We note that the entities covered under the MAT regime are paying Income
Tax as per the MAT rates notified for the respective financial years under the IT Act,
1961, which is levied on the book profit of the entity computed as per Section 115 JB(2)
of the IT Act, 1961.

the net profit in the Statement of Profit and Loss prepared in accordance with the
Order in Petition No. 477/TT/2025
